persons.arguments_against: The strongest counter would be a same-name relative or successor in the Director of Labour post. This collapses immediately: the corpus contains exactly one Amato-Gauci official and one Amato-Gauci biography, so there is no competing same-name candidate; the surname is highly distinctive and hyphenated, not a blocking-key coincidence. Beyond the rare surname, both initials F.E. match, the stint's positions reproduce the bio exactly in matching years -- 'dir. of labr., 1949' = Director of Labour (1953-56), 'dir., emig., labr. and soc. welf., 1956' = Director of Emigration, Labour and Social Welfare (1957-59) -- and the M.B.E. that appears in the 1959 staff-list line matches the bio's 'M.B.E. (mil.) (1945)' (the deterministic honour check failed only on formatting). Age (b.1917, stint from 1953, age 36) is consistent. No realistic counter-hypothesis survives.

evidence_cited.fact: dir., emig., labr. and soc. welf., 1956

persons.would_overturn: Discovery of a second F. E. Amato-Gauci in Malta would reverse this, but the corpus shows only one.

notes: Unique rare surname plus exact position chain, two-initial match, M.B.E. honour, and consistent age. High-confidence match.
